commands.review_summary:
The PR reclassifies title-only Readability output as failed extraction, tries the existing web_fetch provider fallback, then falls back to body-only basic HTML cleanup with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +144, Tests +99. Total +243 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main returns any non-empty Readability text, so a title-only extractor result skips provider and raw fallback. The PR body also supplies live after-fix tool-path output and focused regression coverage for the narrowed branch.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the provider fallback compatibility tradeoff, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix #82685: [Bug]: web_fetch fails to extract full HTML body content
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #82685.

- Treat Readability output that only repeats the page title as a failed body extraction.
- Preserve the existing provider-fallback-before-raw-HTML order for that failed extraction path.
- Keep the public `htmlToMarkdown` helper behavior unchanged and use a runtime-local body extraction helper only for the title-only fallback case.
- Locate the document body with a small tag scanner that skips comments and raw-text containers before body-only cleanup.

## Linked context

- **Issue:** #82685 reports `web_fetch` returning head/title text instead of the visible HTML body when Readability does not produce useful body content.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users can receive page-title metadata instead of the content they asked `web_fetch` to fetch, so downstream agent responses are grounded in the wrong page text.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The extraction decision stays in the runtime `web_fetch` source-of-truth path, where Readability success/failure, configured provider fallback, and raw HTML cleanup are ordered today.
- **What did NOT change:** This does not change the public `htmlToMarkdown` helper, provider registration APIs, provider configuration, fetch networking, content wrapping, truncation, schemas, protocols, or Readability implementation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `web_fetch` no longer returns head/title text instead of body text when the HTML body has visible content and the Readability path does not produce useful body content.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux Node runtime in the PR worktree, calling the actual `createWebFetchTool` path against a live HTTPS HTML response from `httpbingo.org` with proxy-backed network access enabled.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `http_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 https_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 all_proxy= node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module <live web_fetch proof script>`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real `web_fetch` tool call:

```json
{
  "status": 200,
  "contentType": "text/html",
  "extractor": "raw-html",
  "textIncludesBody": true,
  "textIncludesTitleBodyPrefix": false
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live command returned a successful HTML fetch through the real tool path, selected the `raw-html` extractor, included the visible body marker, and did not return a title/body prefix.
- **Post-review parser-safety validation:** Added regression coverage where fake `<body>` tags appear inside script/style/comment content before the real document body. The target web-fetch test file passed with 27 tests.
- **What was not tested:** The original reporter did not provide their exact URL or HTML file, so the live proof uses a minimized HTML response with a visible body marker and the regression coverage pins the title-only Readability branch directly.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high; the issue lacks the reporter's exact HTML, but the changed invariant is narrow and verified through the real `web_fetch` tool path plus targeted regression coverage.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts`
- **Patch quality notes:** The patch-quality warnings are expected because this fix intentionally touches fallback behavior. The current version keeps the provider fallback order intact, keeps the public SDK helper unchanged, and confines body-first cleanup to the runtime-local title-only Readability path. The new default returns only represent malformed/no-body HTML parsing exits; they preserve the existing full-document fallback when no body tag exists.

## Tests and validation

- **Scenario locked in:** Readability returns only the page title, while raw HTML contains fake `<body>` tags inside script/style/comment content before the real document body. The expected behavior is `raw-html` extraction from the real visible body, with fake body text ignored.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The focused web-fetch runtime test exercises the title-only Readability branch, the provider-before-body fallback order, and the parser-safe body selection ClawSweeper flagged without adding unrelated provider or gateway setup.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts`

  ```text
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

   RUN  v4.1.7 [local path redacted]


   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  27 passed (27)
     Start at  11:05:53
     Duration  5.10s (transform 2.38s, setup 612ms, import 3.72s, tests 413ms, environment 0ms)

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 16.68s
  ```

- `git diff --check`

  ```text
  Validation result: pass, exit_code=0, 183ms
  ```

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The source invariant in `web_fetch` was too weak: any non-empty Readability text was treated as successful body extraction, so a title-only parser result bypassed the normal failed-extraction pipeline. That caused head/title metadata to be accepted before the runtime could try the configured provider fallback or raw body cleanup.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The runtime now validates the extraction contract at the decision point: title-only Readability output is classified as failed body extraction, then routed through the same provider fallback pipeline as other Readability failures, and only then through runtime-local body HTML cleanup. The body-only cleanup path now locates the actual document body while skipping fake body tags inside comments and raw-text containers, preserving existing provider and SDK contracts without accepting script/comment text as body content.

## Risk checklist

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: compatibility` remains appropriate because configured `web_fetch` providers can now run for pages whose Readability output is only the title.
- **Risk explanation:** This keeps the documented provider-before-basic-HTML order for failed Readability extraction, but it can change extractor source, latency, and provider cost for configured installs.
- **Why acceptable:** A title-only Readability result is not useful body extraction; routing it through the existing failed-extraction path is narrower than accepting title metadata as body content. The body-only cleanup repair further narrows raw fallback to the actual document body.
- **Out of scope:** This PR does not change provider cost policy, provider fallback order, public APIs, config defaults, schemas, protocols, or documentation. Maintainer acceptance is still needed for the provider fallback compatibility tradeoff.
- **Related open PR / competition scan:** No competing narrower same-contract repair was identified in the current PR discussion; this update addresses the ClawSweeper finding on this existing open PR.

## Current review state

- **Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?**
  - RF-001 `status: waiting on author` label — fixed pending re-review: this update addresses the contributor-facing parser-safety finding that caused the waiting label.
  - RF-002 ClawSweeper P2 “Make body extraction ignore script/style/comment/raw-text `<body>` tokens or use a parser, then add a focused regression test.” — fixed: body detection now scans tags while skipping comments and raw-text containers, with a focused regression test.
  - RF-003 ClawSweeper P2 “Have a maintainer explicitly accept the provider fallback source/cost change before merge.” — maintainer-decision: this PR documents the compatibility/cost tradeoff and leaves acceptance to maintainers.
  - RF-004 ClawSweeper P1 provider fallback source/latency/cost risk — maintainer-decision: this PR intentionally preserves provider fallback before raw body cleanup for title-only Readability failures and documents the compatibility risk above.
  - RF-005 ClawSweeper P2 narrow PR-branch repair plus maintainer acceptance — fixed for the parser-safe repair; maintainer-decision remains for provider fallback compatibility.
  - RF-006 ClawSweeper P2 “Locate the real document body before cleanup.” — fixed: body-only cleanup now slices from the real document body instead of the first raw `<body>` token.
  - RF-007 ClawSweeper requested `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts` — verified: the command passed with 27 tests.
  - RF-008 ClawSweeper requested `git diff --check` — verified: the command passed.
- **Proof request state:** The PR still includes live `web_fetch` tool-path proof for the original user-visible issue. This update adds targeted parser-safety regression proof; a local gateway proof would not exercise the raw HTML body locator more directly than the `createWebFetchTool` path and focused runtime test.
- **What remains for maintainers:** Decide whether the documented provider fallback source/cost change is acceptable for title-only Readability failures.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
